header_summary:
Recent discussions have highlighted significant advancements and updates in the Bitcoin Core project. The development and formal verification of Bitcoin's protocol using Lean4, detailed at [btc-verified](https://github.com/ProofOfKeags/btc-verified), have progressed, providing insights into non-malleable aspects of Bitcoin's architecture such as the Merkle Root. Despite the theoretical advancements, practical limitations remain, acknowledging discrepancies between the btc-verified model and actual Bitcoin Core operations, which could affect real-world applicability.

The release of Bitcoin Core versions v29.4rc1 and v30.3rc1 offers new opportunities for community engagement and testing. Both versions are accessible for download and review, with binaries and source code available at [Bitcoin Core’s official site](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-29.4/test.rc1) and [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/tree/v29.4rc1), respectively. These release candidates allow the community to report issues and contribute to the final adjustments before the official release, enhancing the software’s reliability and performance.

Security analyses of various cryptocurrency transaction schemes reveal differing levels of security under specific conditions, as discussed in an in-depth analysis on schemes like P2TRv2 and P2QR. The analysis, available at [DelvingBitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/public-key-recovery-for-ec-leaves-in-p2mr-bip-360/2603/22), shows that while some schemes offer robust protection, others are vulnerable under typical usage scenarios. This emphasizes the need for careful consideration of security features in transaction protocols, balancing complexity against real-world utility.

recent_posts.title: btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
recent_posts.summary:
- The Bitcoin protocol development in Lean4 has been marked by significant progress.
- Formal Verification using Lean4 allows precise definitions and proofs in Bitcoin's operation.
- Initial results show the non-malleability of the Merkle Root in Bitcoin’s architecture.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary: - Bitcoin Core version v29.4rc1 is now available for download.  - Users can access the source code via a signed tag on GitHub.  - Issues with this release candidate should be reported on the project's GitHub tracker.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v30.3rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version v30.3rc1 is now available for download and testing.
- Testers can report issues via the project's GitHub issues page if problems arise.
- Notification sent to the "Bitcoin Development Mailing List" on Google Groups.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary:
- Version v31.1rc1 was tested successfully on Ubuntu 22.04 with kernel 6.8.0.124.
- The test involved an Intel Core i5 processor, four cores, and 8GB of RAM.
- All 284 functional tests passed, and manual verifications confirmed expected outcomes.

recent_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
recent_posts.summary: - The email evaluates security levels for various cryptocurrency transaction schemes.  - P2TRv2 is vulnerable unless Elliptical Curve Cryptography is disabled.  - P2QR maintains robust protection, failing only after ECC spend.

recent_posts.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
recent_posts.summary: - An error in a proposed idea was identified and subsequently retracted.  - The error's recognition followed a correction from another party, who was thanked.  - This incident underscores the value of peer review in problem-solving efforts.

active_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts.summary:
- The modification to BIP360 mandates at least one merkle path in P2MR transactions.
- It aims to prohibit simplistic script trees to ensure privacy and efficient scripting.
- Stakeholder discussions have shown positivity towards these protocol changes.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a new Bitcoin consensus rule for 64-byte transaction encoding.
- Only blocks with specific 64-byte structural matches will be invalidated to reduce risks.
- Implementation details include a C++ function and guidelines for miners on block construction.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary: - Bitcoin developers discuss enhancing security against quantum threats via new disabling mechanisms.  - Two proposals, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, aim to deactivate EC opcodes preemptively.  - These integrations into consensus rules aim to safeguard the network proactively and reliably.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData introduces a method for handling data within Bitcoin via a soft-fork.
- It permits optional data pruning and allocates storage costs to opt-in operators.
- The design incorporates modifications enhancing block serialization and data validation.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations, not the protocol level.
- Enhancements aim to optimize existing measures without fundamental protocol changes.
- Factors like regulatory concerns affect focus on non-protocol level privacy solutions in Bitcoin.

active_posts.title: SHRINCS: 324-byte stateful post-quantum signatures with static backups
active_posts.summary:
- SHRINCS combines stateful and stateless signature methods for efficiency and robustness.
- It uses an unbalanced XMSS tree and SPHINCS+ to operate in dual modes effectively.
- Seamless transitions between modes protect integrity even when the state is uncertain.

today_in_history_posts.title: Merkle trees and mountain ranges
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Peter Todd recommends BLAKE2b for hashing without padding for single-block data.  - He discusses a root branch block design with fixed nodes and terminal outpointers.  - Analysis predicts slow blockchain validation on hard drives, faster with some memory use.

today_in_history_posts.title: [BOLT Draft] Onion Routing Spec
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rusty Russell suggests changes to Lightning Network's protocol construction.
- His proposal includes joining "header" and "payload" into a 40-byte field.
- Additional updates involve enhancing extension compatibility and replay protection.

today_in_history_posts.title: Bolt 12 Trusted Contacts
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bolt 12 enhances user payments by mimicking traditional apps and adding contact management.
- It proposes sharing identity selectively via `contact_key` in offers or BIP21 URIs.
- Feedback is sought to refine identity features in Bolt 12, enhancing both privacy and functionality.
